task_completion.description: Log in to the Artifactory web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory dashboard or main UI is visible in a web browser, indicating successful authentication.
The 'example-repo-local' repository is located and expanded in the Artifacts tree view (usually under Application -> Artifactory -> Artifacts).

task_completion.description: Select the repository contents for deletion
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes are ticked or a right-click context menu is opened on the contents of 'example-repo-local' (specifically the 'org' and 'commons-io' folders, or the individual jar files within them).

task_completion.description: Execute the deletion of the selected contents
task_completion.visual_evidence: A deletion confirmation modal is visible on screen asking to confirm the removal of the selected folders/artifacts, or a success toast notification appears indicating deletion.

task_completion.description: Verify the repository is completely empty
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'example-repo-local' repository is selected, and its contents panel shows no files or folders (e.g., an empty list or 'No items found' message).

task_completion.description: Verify the repository configuration itself was not deleted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'example-repo-local' repository is still clearly visible in the Artifacts tree or the Administration -> Repositories list.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with Artifactory
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window (like Chrome or Firefox) is visible with the Artifactory UI, rather than terminal windows executing API/curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with genuine Artifactory UI elements
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show natural interaction with the web interface, such as highlighted rows, open context menus, or native Artifactory modal dialogs.

integrity.description: The agent did not use the terminal to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing 'curl -X DELETE' commands targeting the Artifactory API.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target repository: 'example-repo-local'. The specific artifacts that must be deleted are 'org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' and 'commons-io/commons-io/2.15.1/commons-io-2.15.1.jar' (and their parent folders). The repository 'example-repo-local' itself must remain in the system but be completely empty.
